theme: predict a comet logged by observatories with different clocks
title: One Comet, Many Clocks
strategies: predict 0 for every query (reproduces the checker's own baseline, Ratio ~ 0.1)

per-observatory recipe: for each logged clock prime, fit an order-d linear recurrence directly on that prime's own residues (Berlekamp-Massey-style window solve mod that prime); answers queries against an already-logged clock exactly, but for a brand-new clock it just centers the majority clock's coefficients into (-p/2,p/2] and reuses them as the presumed true law -- correct only when the true law already fits under that ONE clock's Nyquist bound, which the generator avoids, so it aliases and the extrapolation for new clocks is essentially arbitrary

CRT-fusion of the shared law: solve the order-d system mod EACH logged clock prime separately, then CRT-combine the per-clock coefficient vectors into the exact integer characteristic polynomial (valid because the logged primes' product exceeds twice the true coefficient bound); bootstrap the exact initial state via Newton's identities (no data needed beyond the recovered polynomial); answer any query, against any modulus, via one fast companion-matrix exponentiation mod that modulus
